prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work or vacation days (not travel planning itself)
- travel_suggestion: seeking travel destination ideas or recommendations (not requesting time off)
- direct_deposit: setting up or managing automated salary/payment deposits into accounts
- payday: asking about salary payment timing, dates, or when payment was/will be received
- pay_bill: paying bills or managing bill payments owed to others
- book_hotel: searching for or booking hotel accommodations (even if reviews are mentioned as criteria)
- restaurant_reviews: specifically seeking opinions or ratings about restaurants

Decision rules:
- If text is about requesting vacation/leave from work, classify as pto_request (not travel_suggestion)
- If text asks when salary was/will be paid or deposited, classify as payday (not direct_deposit)
- If text mentions setting up payment to employees or deposit automation, classify as direct_deposit
- If text mentions finding/booking hotels even with review criteria, classify as book_hotel (not restaurant_reviews)

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work, vacation days, or leave (including expressing desire to take vacation)
- travel_suggestion: seeking travel destination ideas or recommendations for where to go
- direct_deposit: setting up or managing automated salary/payment deposits into accounts
- payday: asking about salary payment timing, dates, or when payment was/will be received
- pay_bill: paying bills or managing bill payments owed to others
- book_hotel: searching for or booking hotel accommodations (even if reviews are mentioned as criteria)
- restaurant_reviews: specifically seeking opinions or ratings about restaurants
- lost_luggage: reporting missing or lost baggage from flights or travel
- report_lost_card: reporting a lost, stolen, or missing credit/debit card

Decision rules:
- If text is about wanting/requesting vacation or time off work (even just expressing the desire), classify as pto_request (not travel_suggestion)
- If text asks when salary was/will be paid or deposited, classify as payday (not direct_deposit)
- If text mentions setting up payment to employees or deposit automation, classify as direct_deposit
- If text mentions finding/booking hotels even with review criteria, classify as book_hotel (not restaurant_reviews)
- If text is about missing/lost luggage or baggage from travel, classify as lost_luggage (not report_lost_card)
- If text is about a lost/stolen/missing credit or debit card, classify as report_lost_card (not lost_luggage)

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
